historical_context.location: Quarterdeck of a Whaling Vessel, Stern View
historical_context.atmosphere: Professional tension, maritime hierarchy, overcast sky

lead_subject.name: The Rochester 3rd Mate
lead_subject.features: Neatly combed brown hair, sharp black eyes, determined jawline, slight skin asymmetricality
lead_subject.action: Gripping a brass sextant with stained fingers, looking toward the horizon
lead_subject.attire: Structured officer's cap with worn edges, wool peacoat, high-collared shirt with minor fraying

secondary_subject.name: The 1st Mate
secondary_subject.features: Deep-set stern eyes, unkempt sideburns, weathered skin with visible pores and sun damage
secondary_subject.action: Distracted, looking at a sea chart with a slight furrow in his brow
secondary_subject.attire: Heavy navy wool officer's coat, oxidized brass buttons with green patina

tertiary_subject.name: The Ship's Carpenter
tertiary_subject.features: Muscular build, sweat-streaked forehead, sawdust caught in a thick beard
tertiary_subject.action: Passing in the background, out-of-focus, carrying a heavy mallet
tertiary_subject.attire: Dirty leather apron, coarse striped shirt with visible weaving texture

cinematography.camera_lens: Petzval portrait lens, 150mm equivalent, authentic optical aberrations at the edges
cinematography.lighting: Natural overcast sky, flat but moody top-lighting, realistic shadows under the eyes and chin
cinematography.composition: Candid maritime scene, accidental framing, shallow depth of field, center focus
cinematography.angle: Eye-level, captured from a slightly unstable deck

aesthetic_specs.textures: Oxidized brass, heavy coarse wool with pilling, authentic skin oil, dried salt on wood, yellowed parchment
aesthetic_specs.color_palette: Muted indigo, dull brass, charcoal, cream-white, charcoal-grey, sepia undertones
aesthetic_specs.film_style: 1880s Albumin print on cabinet card, uneven chemical development, subtle silver mirroring, micro-scratches, authentic paper grain

master_prompt: An authentic, unposed 1880s Cabinet Card photograph of three whaling crew members on a ship's quarterdeck. The image is a genuine historical document, showing the 21-year-old 3rd Mate from Rochester in the foreground, his brown hair slightly messy under his cap, blacks eyes focused on the horizon as he holds a tarnished brass sextant. Beside him, an older 1st Mate is caught mid-conversation, his weathered face showing real sun damage and deep pores. A burly carpenter is visible in the blurry background. The lighting is flat and grey from an overcast sky, creating realistic shadows. The image exhibits authentic period-correct optical flaws: slight motion blur on the background figure, subtle chromatic aberration from the Petzval lens, and uneven sepia toning. The skin has a natural, oily texture with visible dirt and imperfections. No digital smoothness.
negative_prompt: ai-generated, digital art, rendering, 3d, smooth skin, symmetrical face, perfect lighting, vibrant colors, sharpened, plastic, clean clothes, studio lighting, modern camera, watermark, text
